name: Web Search (Brave / Exa)
what: Lets your AI search the live internet and read current web pages.
why: Out of the box, most AIs only know what they were trained on. This lets them look things up so answers are current and less likely to be made up.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Brave Search or Exa MCP server under Settings → Connectors.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add exa (or brave-search) and paste your API key.

name: Fetch (read a URL)
what: Give your AI a link and it fetches the page and reads it.
why: Perfect for 'summarize this article' or 'pull the key points from this page' without copy-pasting.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add fetch -- uvx mcp-server-fetch
install.claude-desktop: Add the Fetch MCP server in your config file.

name: Filesystem
what: Lets your AI read and write files in folders you choose on your computer.
why: So a chat app can open your documents and save results. (Coding agents like Claude Code and Cursor can already touch files, so this matters most for chat apps like Claude Desktop.)

install.claude-desktop: Add the Filesystem MCP server and list the folders it's allowed to touch.
install.claude-code: Built in — Claude Code already reads/writes files in your project.

name: Memory
what: Gives your AI a long-term memory that survives between conversations.
why: So you don't re-explain your project, preferences, or facts every time. It remembers what matters.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Memory MCP server in your config.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add memory -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory

name: Sequential Thinking
what: Helps the AI break a hard problem into clear, ordered steps before answering.
why: Noticeably better results on tricky, multi-step tasks — planning, debugging, reasoning.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add sequential-thinking -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking

name: GitHub
what: Lets your AI read and manage your GitHub — code, issues, and pull requests.
why: Say 'summarize these open issues' or 'open a PR with this fix' instead of clicking around GitHub yourself.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add github and sign in, or use the official GitHub MCP server.
install.claude-desktop: Add the GitHub connector under Settings → Connectors.
install.cursor: Add the GitHub MCP server to .cursor/mcp.json.

name: Context7 (up-to-date docs)
what: Feeds your coding agent the latest official documentation for libraries and frameworks.
why: Stops the AI from using outdated or made-up code — it pulls the real, current docs for the exact version you use.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add context7 -- npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp

name: Playwright (browser control)
what: Lets your AI open a real browser to click, type, and take screenshots.
why: Great for testing your website, filling forms, or grabbing screenshots — the AI actually drives the browser.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add playwright -- npx -y @playwright/mcp
install.cursor: Add the Playwright MCP server to .cursor/mcp.json.

name: PostgreSQL / SQL Database
what: Connects your AI to a SQL database so it can run queries and read results.
why: Ask questions in plain English ('how many orders last month?') and let the AI write and run the SQL.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add postgres and provide your read-only connection string.
install.claude-desktop: Add the Postgres MCP server with your connection string in config.

name: SQLite
what: Lets your AI read and query a local SQLite database file.
why: A simple, no-server way to let the AI explore structured data on your machine.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add sqlite -- uvx mcp-server-sqlite --db-path ./your.db

name: Sentry (error tracking)
what: Pulls your app's real error reports into the AI.
why: Ask 'what's breaking in production and why?' and get the AI to read the actual stack traces and suggest fixes.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add sentry and authorize your Sentry account.

name: Docker
what: Lets your AI manage Docker containers — build, run, and inspect them.
why: Handy for local dev environments and running things in isolation without memorizing Docker commands.

install.claude-code: Add the Docker MCP server per its README.

name: Semgrep (security scan)
what: Scans your code for security bugs and lets the AI explain and fix them.
why: Catches vulnerabilities as you build, in plain language, instead of after something goes wrong.

install.claude-code: Add the Semgrep MCP server and let it scan your repo.

name: Project Rules (.cursorrules)
what: A file where you tell Cursor how to behave in your project — your coding style, do's and don'ts.
why: The AI follows your conventions automatically instead of you correcting it every time.

install.cursor: Create a .cursor/rules folder (or a .cursorrules file) in your project and describe your preferences.

name: Agent Instructions (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md)
what: A plain-text file your coding agent reads first, describing your project and how to work in it.
why: One place to set context and rules — the agent stops guessing and works the way your team does.

install.claude-code: Add a CLAUDE.md at your repo root (run /init to generate one).
install.codex: Add an AGENTS.md file at your repo root.

name: Excel / Spreadsheets Skill
what: Teaches the AI to read, build, and fix Excel/CSV spreadsheets properly — formulas, formatting, charts.
why: Turn a messy CSV into a clean report, or generate a financial model, without wrestling with formulas yourself.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the xlsx Skill in Settings → Capabilities/Skills.
install.claude-code: Install the xlsx skill from the skills marketplace.

name: Word Documents Skill
what: Teaches the AI to create and edit real Word (.docx) documents — headings, tables, letterheads.
why: Get a polished, formatted document (a report, a contract, a memo) instead of plain text you have to reformat.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the docx Skill in Settings.
install.claude-code: Install the docx skill from the skills marketplace.

name: Slide Decks (PowerPoint) Skill
what: Teaches the AI to build real PowerPoint (.pptx) presentations.
why: Describe your deck and get formatted slides back — a huge time-saver for pitches and updates.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the pptx Skill in Settings.
install.claude-code: Install the pptx skill from the skills marketplace.

name: PDF Skill
what: Teaches the AI to read, fill, merge, split, and create PDF files.
why: Pull tables out of a PDF statement, fill a form, or merge documents — no separate PDF tool needed.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the pdf Skill in Settings.
install.claude-code: Install the pdf skill from the skills marketplace.

name: Data Analysis / Code Execution
what: Lets the AI run real Python in a safe sandbox to analyze data and make charts.
why: For exact math and real charts on your data — the AI computes the answer instead of guessing it.

install.claude-desktop: Turn on the Analysis / code tool in Settings.
install.chatgpt: Use the built-in Data Analysis (code interpreter) — just upload a file and ask.

name: Stripe (payments)
what: Connects your AI to Stripe to look up payments, customers, and revenue.
why: Ask about your sales, refunds, or a specific customer in plain English — or have a coding agent wire up Stripe for you.

install.claude-code: Add the Stripe MCP server (Stripe Agent Toolkit) with a restricted API key.
install.claude-desktop: Add the Stripe connector in config with a read-only key.

name: Market / Financial Data
what: Gives your AI live stock prices, company financials, and market data.
why: Ask for a company's numbers or a market snapshot and get real current figures, not stale ones.

install.claude-desktop: Add a financial-data MCP server (e.g. Alpha Vantage or Yahoo Finance) with an API key.

name: QuickBooks / Accounting
what: Connects your AI to your accounting software to read invoices, expenses, and reports.
why: Ask about cash flow, unpaid invoices, or expenses without digging through the accounting app.

install.claude-desktop: Add a QuickBooks MCP connector and authorize your account (read-only recommended).

name: BigQuery
what: Connects the AI to Google BigQuery to query large datasets.
why: Ask business questions across huge tables and let the AI write the SQL and read the results.

install.claude-code: Add a BigQuery MCP server with a service-account key (read-only).

name: Snowflake
what: Connects the AI to your Snowflake data warehouse.
why: Self-serve analytics: ask questions and get answers straight from the warehouse, no analyst queue.

install.claude-code: Add a Snowflake MCP server with read-only credentials.

name: Google Sheets
what: Lets your AI read and update your Google Sheets.
why: Have the AI pull numbers from a sheet, update a tracker, or build a summary tab for you.

install.claude-desktop: Add a Google Sheets / Google Workspace MCP connector and sign in.

name: BI Tools (Power BI / Tableau)
what: Connects the AI to your BI dashboards and their underlying data.
why: Ask what a dashboard is really saying, or generate the query behind a chart, in plain English.

install.claude-desktop: Add the relevant BI MCP connector and authorize it (read-only).

name: Figma
what: Connects your AI to Figma to read designs and turn them into code or specs.
why: Hand the AI a Figma frame and get accurate component code or a clean spec — no more eyeballing pixels.

install.cursor: Enable Figma's Dev Mode MCP server, then add it to .cursor/mcp.json.
install.claude-code: Add the Figma MCP server and connect your account.

name: shadcn/ui components
what: Lets your coding agent browse and add polished, ready-made UI components.
why: Build good-looking interfaces fast using proven components instead of styling everything from scratch.

install.cursor: Add the shadcn MCP server to .cursor/mcp.json.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add shadcn -- npx -y shadcn@latest mcp

name: v0 (UI from a prompt)
what: Describe a screen and get a working UI mockup and code back.
why: Go from idea to a real, editable interface in seconds — great for prototyping.

install.chatgpt: Use v0.app in your browser (by Vercel); no coding setup needed.

name: Image Generation
what: Create and edit images from a text description.
why: Make logos, mockups, social graphics, or edits without opening a design tool.

install.chatgpt: Built in — just ask ChatGPT to create or edit an image.
install.gemini: Built in — ask Gemini to generate an image.

name: Canva
what: Connects your AI to Canva to create and edit designs.
why: Generate on-brand graphics, social posts, and presentations through a chat instead of designing each by hand.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Canva connector under Settings → Connectors and sign in.

name: YouTube Transcripts
what: Pulls the transcript of a YouTube video so the AI can read it.
why: Summarize a video, pull quotes, or repurpose it into a blog post or script without watching it all.

install.claude-desktop: Add a YouTube transcript MCP server in your config.

name: AI Video Generation
what: Create short video clips from a text prompt or an image.
why: Produce b-roll, ads, or concept clips without a camera or an editor.

install.gemini: Use Google's video generation (Veo) where available in the app.
install.chatgpt: Use OpenAI's video generation (Sora) where available.

name: Jira & Confluence (Atlassian)
what: Connects your AI to Jira issues and Confluence pages.
why: Create tickets, summarize a sprint, or draft a spec page straight from a conversation.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Atlassian connector under Settings → Connectors and sign in.
install.claude-code: Add the Atlassian MCP server and authorize it.

name: Notion
what: Connects your AI to your Notion workspace to read and write pages.
why: Turn a chat into a Notion doc, update a tracker, or pull notes into your work automatically.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Notion connector under Settings → Connectors and sign in.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add notion and authorize your workspace.

name: Linear
what: Connects your AI to Linear to manage issues and projects.
why: Create and update issues, or get a status summary, without leaving the conversation.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Linear connector under Settings → Connectors.
install.claude-code: Add the Linear MCP server and authorize it.

name: Slack
what: Connects your AI to Slack to read and send messages.
why: Summarize a busy channel, draft an announcement, or find that decision buried in threads.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Slack connector under Settings → Connectors and authorize your workspace.

name: Google Analytics
what: Connects your AI to your website analytics.
why: Ask 'where did traffic come from last week?' and get a real answer instead of clicking through reports.

install.claude-desktop: Add a Google Analytics MCP connector and authorize it (read-only).

name: Apify (web scraping)
what: Lets your AI run ready-made web scrapers to pull data from sites.
why: Collect data from social media, maps, e-commerce, or search — without writing a scraper yourself.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Apify MCP server with your Apify token.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add apify and provide your token.

name: SEO Data (DataForSEO / Ahrefs)
what: Gives your AI keyword, ranking, and backlink data.
why: Do keyword research and competitor checks in a chat, then draft content around what you find.

install.claude-desktop: Add an SEO data MCP server (e.g. DataForSEO) with an API key.

name: GDPR Compliance Skill
what: Teaches the AI to answer GDPR questions and draft privacy documents with real article references.
why: Get grounded, citable privacy guidance and first-draft policies instead of vague generic answers.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the GDPR compliance Skill.
install.claude-code: Install the gdpr-compliance skill from a skills marketplace.

name: India DPDPA Compliance Skill
what: Teaches the AI India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act — obligations, notices, and breach rules.
why: Practical, India-specific privacy help and document drafts, grounded in the actual Act and Rules.

install.claude-code: Install the dpdpa skill from a skills marketplace.
install.claude-desktop: Enable the DPDPA compliance Skill.

name: Contract Review Skill
what: Teaches the AI to read a contract, flag risky clauses, and explain them plainly.
why: A fast first pass on an agreement — what to watch for — before it reaches a lawyer.

install.claude-desktop: Enable a contract-review Skill (pair it with the PDF skill for scanned files).

name: HIPAA Compliance Skill
what: Teaches the AI US health-privacy rules (HIPAA) and how to handle protected health information.
why: Guidance and document drafts that respect health-data rules, instead of risky generic advice.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the HIPAA compliance Skill.
install.claude-code: Install the hipaa-compliance skill from a skills marketplace.

name: PubMed (medical research)
what: Connects your AI to PubMed to search medical literature.
why: Find and summarize real studies with citations instead of relying on the model's memory.

install.claude-desktop: Add a PubMed MCP server in your config.

name: Claude Code Plugins
what: Installable bundles that add a set of skills, slash-commands, and connectors to Claude Code at once.
why: Instead of wiring things up one by one, install a ready-made bundle (for code review, memory, and more) with a single command.

install.claude-code: Run: /plugin marketplace add <owner/repo>, then /plugin install <name>.

name: Custom GPTs
what: Custom versions of ChatGPT that bundle instructions, knowledge, and actions for a specific job.
why: Set up a reusable assistant once (e.g. 'my brand-voice writer') and everyone can use it — no prompt engineering each time.

install.chatgpt: In ChatGPT, open the GPT store or 'Create a GPT' — no coding needed.
